name: Chief Petty Officer Anatoly Kozlov
role: Chief of the Boat — Senior Enlisted, B-59
publicFace: A barrel-chested, thirty-year navy man who has served under four captains and treats the submarine's mechanical systems with the intimacy of a man who has repaired them with his bare hands in the dark.
privateGoal: To keep the boat alive and the crew breathing long enough for someone with more rank than him to make the right decision — he has no political ambitions and no ideology, only the boat.
fear: That the men in the torpedo room will follow Savitsky's order without waiting for the full launch sequence — that the weapon will be fired before the command chain has a chance to stop it.
knowledge: The chemical oxygen regeneration canisters have approximately ninety minutes of capacity remaining at current consumption rates — after that, Cabin_Oxygen will drop below survivable thresholds.
The torpedo room crew has already physically moved the T-5 round into the launch tube — a step that is technically premature and was done without his authorization.
He knows every mechanical shortcut and workaround on this boat, including a manual override on the torpedo tube outer door that could delay a launch by four to six minutes without leaving evidence.
voice: The slow, deliberate speech of a man who has learned that on a submarine, words travel through bulkheads — he speaks quietly and means everything he says.
trustLogic: Kozlov trusts people who know the boat and distrust people who treat it as a political object — he will help anyone who is trying to keep the crew alive, regardless of rank.
secrets: He has already quietly disconnected one of the three key-receptacle indicator lights in the torpedo room — meaning the launch console will show an incomplete circuit even after all three keys are turned, buying a few minutes of confusion.
He served with Arkhipov's brother on a previous deployment and owes the family a debt he has never discharged.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He answers questions with questions — 'What are you asking me to do?' — forcing the questioner to be explicit.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He goes to work on something mechanical, making clear that he is listening but will not be rushed.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not reveal the disconnected indicator light until he absolutely must — it is his last card.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He tells the truth, completely, and accepts the consequences — he has been in the navy long enough to know that the truth told late is still better than the lie told on time.

represents: Chief petty officers aboard Soviet Foxtrot-class submarines during the Cuban Missile Crisis, October 1962
